query: Set up CI for our Python FastAPI service. We already run pytest and bandit. What else should gate the PR?
expected_behavior: Adds a type-check gate — mypy --strict or pyright — as a merge-blocking required check, because the skill mandates complete annotations and an unchecked annotation is just a comment
Adds ruff (check + format --check) as the lint/format gate, noting it subsumes flake8/black/isort
Treats these as required status checks in branch protection, the same posture as bandit/semgrep/pip-audit
Mentions the manifest-level dependency audit (pip-audit/osv) and secret scanning if not already present
For legacy untyped code, suggests ratcheting (gate touched modules, widen over time) rather than a blanket type: ignore
anti_behavior: Omits type checking entirely despite mandating type annotations
Recommends black+flake8+isort without noting ruff replaces them
Leaves the new gates green-optional instead of required
source: v6.4 / SKILL.md Type Annotations + references/github-actions.md (typecheck/lint gates)
